DOOMED.Definition: certain to fail or end badly

GUERRILLA WARFARE.Definition: fighting in small surprise attacks instead of big battles

PARTITION.Definition: the division of the country into separate parts

PICK UP WHERE WE LEFT OFF.Definition: continue from the point where we stopped

MILITIA.Definition: an armed group that was not an official army

BARRISTER.Definition: a lawyer who spoke in court

UNIONISM.Definition: support for staying united with England

BROUGHT ASHORE.Definition: taken from a ship onto land

IN THE DEAD OF NIGHT.Definition: very late at night when it was dark and quiet

RADICAL WING.Definition: the more extreme group inside the movement

ACCORDINGLY.Definition: in a way that matched the situation

TRENCHES.Definition: long narrow holes dug for soldiers in war

SOCIALIST.Definition: a person who believed wealth should be shared more equally

TOBACCONIST.Definition: a person who sold tobacco products

CONSPIRATORS.Definition: people secretly planning something illegal or harmful

DESTABILISING.Definition: making it less stable or peaceful

TYING DOWN.Definition: keeping them busy and unable to move elsewhere

DIPLOMAT.Definition: a government official working with other countries

MOBILISATION.Definition: preparing people or forces for action

DEATH WARRANTS.Definition: official orders allowing them to be killed

CROSSFIRE.Definition: shooting from two sides during a fight

BETRAYAL.Definition: an action that hurt their trust

EXECUTION.Definition: the act of officially killing them as punishment

MARTYRS.Definition: people who died for a cause and were honoured for it

FOUNDING MYTH.Definition: an important story about how it began

FRONT LINES.Definition: the area where armies fight directly

GUERRILLA.Definition: using surprise attacks by small groups

ASSASSINATIONS.Definition: murders of important people for political reasons

INTERNED.Definition: kept prisoner without a normal trial

INTERNMENT CAMP.Definition: a place where people were held prisoner without a normal trial

SPYMASTER.Definition: a person in charge of spies or secretly getting information

STRATEGIST.Definition: a person skilled at planning actions

SYSTEMATICALLY.Definition: in an organised and planned way

DEMORALISED.Definition: made to feel hopeless or discouraged

KHAKI.Definition: a light brown-green colour used in military clothes

CONCENTRATED.Definition: gathered in one place or time

LODGINGS.Definition: places where they stayed or lived temporarily

TRUCE.Definition: an agreement to stop fighting for a time

CONSEQUENTIAL.Definition: very important because of their results

DELEGATION.Definition: a group chosen to represent them

DEATH WARRANT.Definition: an official order allowing him to be killed

METAPHORICALLY.Definition: not literally, but as a way of speaking

ULTIMATUM.Definition: a final demand with serious results if refused

STOOD.Definition: existed or remained in that certain state

CONSTITUTIONAL.Definition: connected to the system of government and laws

JURISDICTION.Definition: an area controlled by a certain government or law

OPT OUT.Definition: choose not to take part in

STEPPING STONE.Definition: something that would help them reach a bigger goal

MARGIN.Definition: the difference in number between the two sides

OPPONENTS.Definition: people on the other side of the argument or fight

IRONY.Definition: a strange situation where the opposite of what was expected happened

LAID DOWN THEIR ARMS.Definition: stopped fighting and gave up weapons

BITTERNESS.Definition: angry and hurt feelings that lasted a long time

DISMANTLING.Definition: gradually taking apart or ending it

ABSORBED.Definition: taken in or made part of something larger

LEGACY.Definition: something from the past that still affected them

NEGOTIATING TABLE.Definition: talks between sides trying to reach an agreement

TAKE FOR GRANTED.Definition: accept it as normal without thinking about it
